labs.title: How Deutsche Telekom is rewiring telecommunications with AI
labs.summary: How Deutsche Telekom is becoming an AI-native telco with OpenAI-transforming customer service, employee workflows, network operations, and the future of voice.

labs.title: GPT-5.6 is now the preferred model in Microsoft 365 Copilot
labs.summary: Learn how GPT-5.6 powers Microsoft 365 Copilot with stronger AI capabilities across Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Chat, and Cowork for faster, higher-quality work.

labs.title: GPT-5.6: Frontier intelligence that scales with your ambition
labs.summary: More intelligence from every token, stronger performance per dollar, and more capability on demand for your hardest work.

Across model variants, SensorFM-B wins on 33 of 35 tasks. The tasks span six categories: cardiovascular health, metabolic risk, mental health, sleep,…

labs.title: Our approach to government and national security partnerships
labs.summary: Learn how OpenAI approaches government and national security partnerships, with principles for responsible AI use, democratic accountability, and public safety.

labs.title: Separating signal from noise in coding evaluations
labs.summary: A new analysis from OpenAI reveals issues in SWE-Bench Pro, a popular coding benchmark, raising concerns about reliability and accuracy in evaluating AI models.

startups.title: Reed Jobs would rather talk about curing cancer than his last name
startups.summary: What undruggable targets are your companies going after? We’re going after it with three different companies and several strategies. Three years into running Yosemite, what’s been the biggest surprise?

startups.title: This slushie machine was a lifesaver during NYC’s heat wave
startups.summary: Last weekend’s brutal NYC heat wave had me craving a frozen drink almost every afternoon. This time, though, I stayed home and put the new Ninja Slushi Twist to the test. For entertaining, this machine solves a real problem.

startups.title: Smart glasses without a camera? Even Realities bets productivity beats recording everyone
startups.summary: Even Realities’ G2 smart glasses are in the same boat. Even Realities takes a different approach to smart glasses than players like Meta. Smart glasses are coming out fast.

startups.title: OpenAI bets on families as ChatGPT goes deeper into households
startups.summary: More than three years after ChatGPT’s launch brought generative AI into the mainstream, OpenAI is broadening its focus beyond individual users to families. OpenAI is hiring a dedicated product manager in San Francisco to build experiences for families, caregivers, and older adults across its products. The hiring comes as ChatGPT’s audience continues to broaden beyond younger users.

startups.title: US cybersecurity agency CISA had to build its incident playbook during the incident, agency reveals
startups.summary: U.S. federal cybersecurity agency CISA said it did not have a prepared response plan for how it should handle a cybersecurity incident in May, after an investigative reporter notified the agency that a contractor had publicly exposed sensitive keys and credentials for accessing U.S. government systems. Only after Krebs contacted CISA did the agency take the repository offline and revoke and replace all of the…

startups.title: Phia accused of ‘cookie stuffing,’ taking affiliate credit on purchases it didn’t earn
startups.summary: Phia, the shopping startup co-founded by Bill Gates’ daughter, Phoebe Gates, and Sophia Kianni, has been accused of a practice known as “cookie stuffing,” which may have helped the product receive commissions and credit for sales it did not actually generate, according to a Bloomberg investigation . The report has sparked controversy and led to Phia’s suspension from Impact.com , a leading affiliate and influencer…

startups.title: Meta removes controversial AI feature on Instagram after backlash
startups.summary: Meta has axed a controversial feature that allowed users to modify photos from public Instagram accounts using AI. Meta promoted one feature that allowed individuals to generate images by @-mentioning public Instagram accounts that they wanted to reference. Now Meta has reversed course.

startups.title: Bluesky’s interim CEO, Toni Schneider, drops the ‘interim’
startups.summary: In March, Bluesky’s longtime CEO, Jay Graber, stepped down from that role to become its chief innovation officer. Graber was immediately succeeded by Toni Schneider, the founding CEO of Automattic, which is the company behind WordPress and Tumblr. “I’m four months into my interim CEO role at Bluesky, and it’s time for an update,” Schneider wrote on his personal blog .

startups.title: Apple sues OpenAI over alleged trade secret theft
startups.summary: Apple filed a lawsuit Friday against OpenAI over allegations of trade secret theft and breach of contract. Before joining OpenAI, Tan had spent 24 years at Apple, most recently as VP of product design for the iPhone and Apple Watch. OpenAI was asked for comment.

startups.summary: Fizz’s filing also alleges that Lu continued to act as a conduit, funneling information about Fizz’s fundraising efforts and other matters to Sidechat. However, Fizz claims Lu had been in discussions with Sidechat as early as 2022. Fizz declined to comment.

startups.title: SK Hynix raises $26.5B in the biggest foreign IPO in US history, is urged to build new US fabs
startups.summary: SK Hynix, a South Korean memory chip giant, said Friday it has raised $26.5 billion (KRW 40 trillion) in its U.S. market debut. But SK Hynix clearly isn’t suffering from the Korea Discount and that’s because it makes memory chips, including high-bandwidth memory (HBM). IPO for SK Hynix: Both Korean chipmakers just pledged more than $550 billion for new manufacturing investment in South Korea.

startups.title: A new app, HyperTexting, turns the open web into a scrollable social media-like feed
startups.summary: A new app called HyperTexting is making it as easy to surf the web as it is to scroll through a social media feed, like Facebook or X. That, of course, changed with the arrival of social media. Most prefer a scrollable feed — the kind that social media sites use.

wider_tech.title: Prefer strict tables in SQLite
wider_tech.summary: SQLite has a feature that I think is underrated: strict tables . Strict tables help enforce rigid typing, preventing mistakes like putting text into integer columns. Personally, I think the pros of strict tables outweigh the cons. (280 points, 131 comments on Hacker News)

Source: YCharts For the full-year, CoreWeave expects to spend $31 billion to $35 billion on capex, or $33 billion at the midpoint. Given CoreWeave’s $2.27 billion cash balance, this creates a huge funding gap of $17.33 billion. (253 points, 87 comments on Hacker News)

Pfendler, who launched from Monterey, California, in May, set out to become the first American woman, youngest woman and… (292 points, 100 comments on Hacker News)

wider_tech.title: We scaled PgBouncer to 4x throughput
wider_tech.summary: PgBouncer is single-threaded. A single process uses one CPU core, no matter how many the machine has. One pooler box ran a single PgBouncer process; the other ran a fleet of 16. (206 points, 44 comments on Hacker News)
